svg.text: 16.3 · Idempotency for money — exactly-once in effect
the load-bearing primitive: every money operation safely retryable · key + effect ATOMIC (M07/M12/12.9)
FIRST request (K7 new) — ONE atomic txn (M07):
INSERT key K7 (UNIQUE) + debit + credit + ledger entries (16.2) → apply once, record result, return it
key + effect atomic → a crash between them can't double-apply
RETRY (same K7) — UNIQUE CONSTRAINT fails → "already processed"
→ do NOT re-apply · return the RECORDED result → charged exactly ONCE, no double-charge
the database enforces dedup atomically (the unique index)
you can't prevent duplicate DELIVERY (exactly-once delivery is a myth, M12/12.13) — make duplicate PROCESSING harmless
at-least-once delivery (never lost) + idempotent processing (duplicates no-op) = exactly-once EFFECT
the primitive under transfers, Saga steps (M12/12.8), CDC consumers (M12/12.13) · non-negotiable for money
💰 the single most important money-never-lies protection at the request level — NO double-charge
+ the atomic ledger (16.2) + reconciliation (16.7) → every payment moves money exactly once, provably
